title: alyssum diffusum subsp. garganicum
intro: Alyssum diffusum subsp. garganicum, also known as Garganic alyssum or Garganica, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine and is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential in treating various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
